skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion component code into MP4 videos through the inference.sh rendering service.
skill.summary: Convert Remotion TSX components into rendered MP4 videos with configurable video settings.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly Markdown examples and inline code, not Ruby backtick execution. The skill still intentionally uses the belt CLI to send TSX code to inference.sh for rendering, so publication should include a clear third-party execution and data transfer warning.

medium_findings.title: External Rendering Service Receives User Code
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run belt app run infsh/remotion-render with TSX code in the input. This is intended functionality, but it transfers source code, props, and video content to an external service for processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented command directly invokes the inference.sh app and includes a code field. This confirms intentional third-party processing, although no malicious endpoint is shown.

medium_findings.title: Remote Execution Trust Boundary
medium_findings.description: The input schema requires React component TSX code and the examples render that code through the remote app. Users must trust the service sandbox and should avoid submitting secrets or private project code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill repeatedly shows user-supplied TSX being submitted to a remote renderer. The risk is a trust-boundary concern rather than evidence of local command injection.

low_findings.title: External Installation Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill recommends installing related skills and the belt CLI using npx commands. These are documentation examples, but users should review packages before installing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are explicit and easy to verify in the documentation. They are not automatically executed by the skill file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: The file links to inference.sh, a hosted image, Remotion documentation, and inference.sh documentation. These links are expected for this skill, but they represent external network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly present in Markdown links and image references. They are not hidden and do not show credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts at the cited lines match ordinary prose, table text, or front matter content. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or another weak cryptographic algorithm being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found descriptive metadata and a Markdown table header, not cryptographic code. The skill file contains no executable crypto implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI Invocation of Third-Party Renderer
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill's core workflow uses belt app run infsh/remotion-render. This is a legitimate external command pattern for the skill, but it should be treated as a networked execution workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The repeated examples show the same external CLI execution pattern. The pattern is intentional and bounded to belt, but it crosses a third-party service boundary.

content.user_title: Render Remotion Videos from React Code
content.value_statement: Teams often need repeatable video generation without building a full rendering pipeline. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code submit Remotion TSX to inference.sh and receive MP4 output.
actual_capabilities: Submits Remotion component code to the infsh/remotion-render app through the belt CLI.
Supports video settings such as width, height, FPS, duration, and codec.
Documents common Remotion imports, including AbsoluteFill, Sequence, spring, and interpolate.
Shows examples for fade-in text, counters, spring motion, props, and sequence timing.
Provides Python SDK examples for standard and streaming render workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends component code and input data to an external rendering service.
Only supports code compatible with the remote Remotion renderer.
Rendering time, availability, and output limits depend on inference.sh.

use_cases.title: Generate Product Launch Videos
use_cases.description: Create short branded clips from reusable Remotion components and product copy.

use_cases.title: Render Data-Driven Motion Graphics
use_cases.description: Turn metrics, counters, and animated charts into repeatable video assets.

use_cases.title: Automate React Video Exports
use_cases.description: Use scripted prompts and SDK calls to render MP4 files from TSX components.

prompt_templates.title: Render a Simple Intro
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short Remotion intro with a title fade-in, then render it as a 1080p MP4.

prompt_templates.title: Customize Video Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Render this Remotion component with 1920 by 1080 resolution, 30 FPS, and a 6 second duration.

prompt_templates.title: Use Dynamic Props
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this Remotion component to accept title and subtitle props, then render a preview video with my provided copy.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Sequenced Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-scene Remotion animation using Sequence, spring, and interpolate, then render the final MP4.

output_examples.input: A request to render a three second title card with fade-in text.
output_examples.output: A rendered MP4 URL or file reference for the generated title card.

output_examples.input: A Remotion component with product name and tagline props.
output_examples.output: A customized product video rendered with the provided text values.

output_examples.input: A request for streaming progress during a longer render.
output_examples.output: Progress updates followed by the final rendered video location.

best_practices: Remove secrets, private source code, and unreleased assets before remote rendering.
Start with short durations and lower resolution before rendering final output.
Keep Remotion components deterministic so repeated renders produce consistent videos.
anti_patterns: Do not submit credentials, tokens, or confidential customer data in props or code.
Do not expect local-only rendering because the workflow uses inference.sh.
Do not render unreviewed third-party TSX without checking imports and asset URLs.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders React and Remotion component code into MP4 video output through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the belt CLI and the infsh/remotion-render app.

faq.question: Can I configure video size and duration?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented inputs include width, height, FPS, duration, and codec.

faq.question: Can it use component props?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a props object for passing values into the default component.

faq.question: Is private code safe to submit?
faq.answer: Treat submitted code as shared with a third-party service. Remove secrets and sensitive content first.

faq.question: Does it include local rendering code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation for running the remote renderer through CLI or SDK examples.
